title: senecio jacobaea var. flosculosus
intro: Senecio jacobaea var. flosculosus, also known as Tansy Ragwort, is a biennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Senecio jacobaea var. flosculosus include its potential to treat conditions such as indigestion, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural insecticide. In herbal medicine, Senecio jacobaea var. flosculosus is often prepared as an infusion, tea, or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the risk of liver damage with long-term use.
